meta.title: Software Hiring Pipeline
meta.subtitle: from sourcing to onboarding · today vs. AI-augmented future

nodes.title: Open requisition
nodes.objective: Hiring manager defines the role, level, target start date, and gets sign-off on headcount.
nodes.entity: Job requisition · Job description · Comp band
nodes.actors: HM drafts → Recruiter reviews → Approver signs off on headcount
nodes.triggers: Team growth need · Backfill · New project
nodes.next: Sourcer starts outbound; recruiter posts publicly.

today.narrative: HM writes the JD from scratch in a doc, often copying from old roles. Recruiter rewrites for clarity. Comp band looked up manually in spreadsheet. Sign-off chain is email/Slack.

tomorrow.narrative: AI drafts the JD from a 3-line brief + role template + recent successful hires. Comp band auto-populated from market data. Sign-off triggers programmatically with approval chain.
proven_pattern: JD generators in production at multiple ATSes

nodes.title: Source candidates
nodes.objective: Build a top-of-funnel pipeline of qualified candidates through outbound search, referrals, and inbound applications.
nodes.actors: Sourcer drives outbound → Recruiter reviews inbound → HM gives 'thumbs up' on shortlist
nodes.triggers: Open requisition exists. Target persona defined.
nodes.next: Qualified candidates move to phone screen.

today.narrative: Sourcer searches LinkedIn manually with boolean queries. Outreach templates pasted from a doc. Replies tracked in spreadsheet or basic ATS. Referrals come in via Slack.

tomorrow.narrative: AI scores candidates against the JD + past hires, drafts personalized outreach per candidate, A/B tests subject lines, follows up automatically. Sourcer reviews + approves; doesn't write.
proven_pattern: AI sourcing tools shipping at scale (HireEZ, SeekOut)

nodes.title: Apply / respond
nodes.objective: Candidate either responds to outreach or applies via job board.
nodes.actors: Candidate submits → ATS captures → Recruiter notified
nodes.triggers: Outreach received OR job posting discovered.

today.narrative: Standard ATS application form. Resume parsed automatically. Confirmation email sent.

tomorrow.narrative: Candidate has a conversation with an AI agent that asks the right follow-up questions, captures structured info beyond the resume.

nodes.title: Resume screen
nodes.objective: Filter the candidate pool down to a manageable set worth phone-screening.
nodes.actors: Recruiter reads → applies rubric → HM spot-checks edge cases
nodes.triggers: New application received OR sourced candidate replied positively.
nodes.next: Pass → phone interview scheduled. Reject → rejection email sent.

today.narrative: Recruiter reads each resume (3-5 min each). Quick decision: pass/reject/maybe. 'Maybe' pile reviewed by HM weekly. Rejection emails sent manually or via template.

tomorrow.narrative: AI scores resumes against the rubric + past successful hires, surfaces edge cases for human review, generates structured reject reasons. Recruiter reviews only borderline cases.
proven_pattern: Resume screening AI in many enterprise ATSes

nodes.title: Schedule phone screen
nodes.objective: Find a time that works for candidate + interviewer.
nodes.actors: Recruiter coordinates → Candidate confirms → Calendar invite sent

today.narrative: Back-and-forth email/Slack to find time. Sometimes Calendly link. Cancellations cause re-scheduling chains.

tomorrow.narrative: Self-serve booking link with interviewer pool availability. Auto-rebook on cancellation. No human in the loop unless something breaks.

nodes.title: Phone screen
nodes.objective: Validate motivation, basic fit, salary expectations, and answer candidate's questions about the role.
nodes.entity: Screen notes · Rubric scorecard · Salary expectations
nodes.actors: Recruiter leads → Candidate answers + asks → Notes captured
nodes.next: Pass → onsite loop scheduled. Reject → kind rejection.

today.narrative: Recruiter takes notes by hand or in ATS during the call. Scorecard filled in after. Salary expectations captured verbally.

tomorrow.narrative: AI transcribes the call, extracts answers to standard questions, auto-fills the scorecard, flags inconsistencies or red flags. Recruiter reviews/edits — doesn't type.

nodes.title: Take phone screen
nodes.objective: Candidate evaluates the company while being evaluated.
nodes.actors: Candidate joins → answers questions → asks their own

nodes.title: Schedule onsite
nodes.objective: Coordinate 5 interviewers + candidate for a single block (in-person or remote).
nodes.actors: Recruiter coordinates → Interviewers confirm → Candidate confirms

today.narrative: Recruiter pings 5 interviewers individually to confirm. Builds the agenda in Google Calendar manually. Sends candidate prep doc.

tomorrow.narrative: Interviewer pool with availability windows; system auto-builds the loop based on roles needed. Candidate prep doc auto-generated for the specific company + role.

nodes.title: Run interviews
nodes.objective: Gather structured signal across 4-5 dimensions: coding, design, behavioral, role-specific, culture.
nodes.actors: Interviewer prepares → conducts → debriefs in scorecard
nodes.next: Each interviewer submits a scorecard with a hire/no-hire vote and rationale.

today.narrative: Interviewer prepares from a question bank doc. Asks questions, takes notes by hand. After interview, types up scorecard (often days late). Vote: strong hire / hire / no-hire / strong no-hire.

tomorrow.narrative: AI suggests questions based on candidate's resume + the role's signal needs. Transcribes the interview. Generates structured scorecard draft from the transcript. Interviewer reviews + commits within 1 hour, not 1 week.

nodes.title: Run the loop
nodes.objective: Candidate performs across 5 interviews over a day.

nodes.title: Debrief meeting
nodes.objective: Panel reads all scorecards, discusses each interview, converges on a recommendation.
nodes.actors: HM facilitates → Interviewers present → Recruiter captures notes
nodes.triggers: All scorecards submitted (target: within 24h of last interview).

today.narrative: 60-min meeting on calendar. HM reads scorecards aloud, each interviewer adds color. Discussion is conversational. Decision often delayed because scorecards are late.

tomorrow.narrative: AI synthesizes scorecards into a 1-page debrief brief BEFORE the meeting (key signals, divergences, risk areas). Debrief becomes a 20-min discussion focused on the divergences, not on re-reading.
proven_pattern: Debrief summarization shipping in modern ATSes

nodes.title: Approve / reject
nodes.objective: Final hiring authority reviews panel recommendation, checks comp + level calibration, makes the call.
nodes.actors: Approver reviews packet → asks questions → decides
nodes.next: Approve → offer prepared. Reject → kind rejection.

today.narrative: Approver reads scorecards + debrief notes. Often asks for additional reference checks or pings the HM for clarification. Comp decided in a separate conversation.

tomorrow.narrative: AI packet includes: panel recommendation, calibration vs past hires at this level, market comp range, risk flags from interviews, suggested comp offer. Approver decides faster, with more context.
proven_pattern: Calibration tooling at Stripe, Meta, others

nodes.title: Reference checks
nodes.objective: Validate the panel's read on the candidate through 2-3 backchannel references.
nodes.actors: Recruiter contacts → references respond → notes attached to packet
nodes.triggers: Approver requests OR strong-hire recommendation needs validation.
nodes.next: Notes go into the approval packet.

today.narrative: Recruiter emails references, schedules 20-min calls, takes notes by hand. Slow — often takes 3-5 days.

tomorrow.narrative: Async reference platform (references answer structured questions in writing). AI synthesizes responses. Same-day turnaround.

nodes.title: Prepare offer
nodes.objective: Build the offer package: base, equity, signing bonus, benefits, start date.
nodes.entity: Offer letter · Comp package · Equity grant
nodes.actors: Recruiter drafts → Approver signs off → Legal reviews → Offer letter generated

today.narrative: Recruiter pulls comp from comp band sheet, equity from grant table, generates letter from template. Manual data entry. Legal reviews each one.

tomorrow.narrative: Offer auto-generated from approved package. Legal templates pre-blessed for standard cases. DocuSign auto-sent.
proven_pattern: Offer letter automation in modern ATSes

nodes.title: Extend offer
nodes.objective: Recruiter (or HM) calls candidate, walks through the offer, answers questions, sells the role.
nodes.entity: Offer call · Verbal acceptance OR negotiation
nodes.actors: Recruiter calls → Candidate responds → HM joins if needed

today.narrative: Recruiter schedules a 30-min call with the candidate. Walks through offer. Answers questions. Sometimes HM joins to sell the role.

tomorrow.narrative: Stays human — the offer call is a relationship moment, not a transaction.
proven_pattern: No automation here is good automation.

nodes.title: Decide
nodes.objective: Candidate evaluates the offer against alternatives, makes a decision.
nodes.next: Accept → onboarding kicks off. Decline → loss-reason captured. Negotiate → back to recruiter.

nodes.title: Onboard
nodes.objective: From accepted offer to productive Day 1: equipment, accounts, paperwork, first-week schedule.
nodes.entity: Onboarding checklist · Equipment order · Account provisioning · Day 1 schedule
nodes.actors: Recruiter hands off to People Ops → IT provisions → HM plans Week 1

today.narrative: Onboarding checklist in a doc. Equipment ordered manually. Account creation requested in tickets. HM builds Week 1 schedule from a template.

tomorrow.narrative: Acceptance triggers automated pipeline: equipment ordered, accounts provisioned, Slack channels added, buddy assigned, Week 1 calendar built. HM just shows up Day 1.
